topic: Someone discovers their recipe has become famous under another name
raw_output:
[story 1]

Eleanor stared at the trending topics on her phone, a slow, tight knot forming in her chest. Every time she scrolled past the latest viral recipe, it invariably ended with a picture of a ridiculously elaborate, slightly altered version of her grandmother’s simple lemon drizzle cake. "Oh, for heaven's sake," she muttered, tapping furiously. She had spent three hours perfecting that recipe—the one with the specific ratio of zest to sugar—only for some influencer named 'SweetSeraphina' to claim it as hers, complete with a completely different, overly complicated presentation. Eleanor slammed her phone onto the counter, the ceramic clinking loudly. She pushed a hand through her already messy hair, the frustration bubbling up like hot water. It was just so unfair.

[story 2]

Mark sighed, running a hand over his temples as he watched the comments section of the baking forum explode. His eyes narrowed as he read the breathless praise directed at 'The Golden Crumb.' He remembered the precise steps he’d meticulously documented on his personal blog—the secret ingredient was a pinch of smoked paprika, something nobody ever mentioned. Now, 'The Golden Crumb' was getting millions of views, lauded for a recipe that, in essence, was his. He leaned back in his chair, the springs groaning under his weight. He picked up his own cookbook, the pages feeling suddenly heavy and meaningless. He just wanted the quiet satisfaction of a well-executed bake, not this bizarre sense of being overshadowed by a phantom chef.

[story 3]

"Seriously?" Clara whispered, the sound sharp in the quiet kitchen. She picked up the newspaper, the headline screaming about the unprecedented success of 'The Velvet Whisk.' Her jaw tightened. She had perfected the sourdough starter, the slow fermentation, the exact temperature control—all painstakingly documented in her private notes. Yet, the public was celebrating a version of her bread that seemed subtly, infuriatingly, tweaked. Clara paced the length of the room, her heels clicking a rapid, impatient rhythm on the tile floor. She stopped abruptly, glaring at the glossy photo in the paper, a deep, simmering irritation radiating from her posture.
